notes: DSR 6 reports the home cursor position as row 1 column 1.
DSR 6 reports the deterministic CUP position requested by the host.
DSR 6 reports a mid-screen cursor position.
DSR 6 reports the lower-right 24x80 screen cell.

Two back-to-back DSR 6 queries must leave the RAM cursor unchanged and return two exact CPR replies. This formerly documented issue #24: synchronous reply gaps corrupted the second query, printed a stray final byte, and lost its reply. Division-free formatting and absolute TDR stores close the report path's local stalls, while interrupt-driven RX captures overlapping input and the TX ring returns both replies in order.
Primary DA identifies the terminal as a base VT100.

Seven primary-DA queries are each followed immediately by an unsupported private mode and a marker byte in one transport window. Every DA reply and marker must survive while the private CSI is consumed without literal residue. Repetition makes the synchronous-reply RX-loss race deterministic without exceeding the 96-byte pacing window.
Secondary DA (ESC[>c) reports a VT220-family identity with a zero version field.
Tertiary DA (ESC[=c) is consumed cleanly -- the '=' and 'c' never print and the parser resumes normal rendering. The DCS-style tertiary reply is intentionally not implemented (see follow-ups).

Tertiary DA (ESC[=c) is swallowed silently -- it must not emit a reply on the wire, in particular no spurious primary DA (ESC[?1;0c) or secondary DA (ESC[>1;0;0c). Locks the wire behavior that the screen-only sibling case cannot see.

ENQ (0x05) elicits the terminal's answerback message. This terminal has no user-programmable answerback memory, so it returns a short fixed identity string (A2VT100) over the wire. Discriminating: if ENQ is dropped again the wire carries only the harness readiness CPR, which strips to empty and fails this exact match. Marked profile because the answerback content is a documented local default, not spec-mandated.
DECRQM for DECCKM should report reset; firmware currently sends no DECRPM reply.
DECRQSS for SGR should return the current rendition; DCS strings are not implemented.
